welcomeDesc: Create a new Root CA or select one from the left panel.
noCAs: No CAs yet. Click '+ New' to create one.
deleteConfirm: Delete certificate "{cn}" (serial: {serial})? This cannot be undone.
subject: Subject
generating: Generating CA... (RSA 4096-bit key may take a moment)
helpIntro: Understand the core concepts behind the Private CA Manager.
helpWhatIsCADesc: A Certificate Authority (CA) is a trusted entity that issues and manages digital certificates. It verifies the identity of certificate applicants and digitally signs certificates with its private key.
helpWhatIsRootCADesc: A Root CA is the trust anchor of a certificate chain, holding a self-signed certificate. It is the top-level entity in a PKI hierarchy. This tool creates self-signed Root CAs.
helpWhatIsCertDesc: A digital certificate binds a public key to an identity (such as a domain name or organization). It is signed by a CA. Browsers and operating systems use built-in root certificates to verify the chain of trust.
helpWhatIsSANDesc: Subject Alternative Name (SAN) is an X.509 certificate extension that allows a single certificate to protect multiple domain names and IP addresses. Modern browsers require the SAN field.
helpWhatIsEKUDesc: Extended Key Usage (EKU) restricts a certificate's purpose: serverAuth for TLS/SSL server authentication, clientAuth for client authentication.
helpWhatIsSerial: What is a Certificate Serial Number?
helpWhatIsSerialDesc: A unique identifier assigned by the CA to each certificate. Used in Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) to identify revoked certificates. This tool uses 8-byte random hex values.
helpWhatIsValidityDesc: The time window during which a certificate is considered valid. Short periods increase operational overhead, long periods reduce security. Browsers warn on certificates exceeding 398 days.
helpFieldCN: Common Name (CN) is the primary domain name the certificate protects, e.g. example.com. Modern browsers prioritize SAN over CN.
helpFieldOrg: Organization (O) is the legal entity name of the certificate applicant. Used for enterprise/OV certificate validation.
helpFieldCountry: Country code is a 2-letter ISO 3166-1 code, e.g. US for United States, CN for China.
helpFieldSANDNS: DNS Names (SAN - DNS) list all domain names the certificate needs to protect, one per line, e.g. example.com and www.example.com. Browsers validate sites against this field.
helpFieldSANIP: IP Addresses (SAN - IP) list all IP addresses the certificate needs to protect. Useful for internal services without domain names.
helpFieldEKU: Extended Key Usage (EKU) determines the certificate's use case: server authentication (website TLS) or client authentication (user/device identity).
helpFieldValidity: Certificate validity period in days. For production, keep under 398 days (browser limit). Internal tools may use longer periods.
